methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $5.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Commonwealth Fusion Systems is a Series B climate and energy company founded by Bob Mumgaard that has raised $2.863B to date and carries a stated post-money valuation of $5.0B+. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of nine comparables: one public company (LNZA) and eight private firms in the same sector and stage, including Pano AI ($500M), Sage Geosystems ($400M), Reverion ($310M), CarbonCapture Inc. ($300M), and Cemvita Factory ($120M).

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical Series B-to-next-stage step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the last post-money value of $5.0B, yielding an implied valuation of $9.5B. The peer-median method examines the five nearest same-sector, same-stage comparables, which span a range of $120M to $500M and anchor at a median of $310M. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and capital intensity across the climate-tech peer set.

The triangulation across both methods produces a range of $264M (low) to $10.9B (high), with a median implied valuation of $4.9B. The extreme width of this range reflects material tension between stage-progression signals—which support valuations near the current mark—and peer comparables, which suggest substantially lower valuations. Key uncertainties include whether Commonwealth's technology roadmap and capital requirements justify a premium to peer medians, and the degree to which frontier-stage climate ventures merit stage-typical multiples.
